_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning about how books work — which is the front and back, that we read from left to right, and that words are separated by spaces.

_source.w: We are learning about how books work — which is the front and back, that we read from left to right, and that words are separated by spaces.
y: Knowing how books are organised helps us follow along when we read.
Understanding these patterns is essential for becoming an independent reader.
Every book follows the same rules, so once we learn them, we can read any book.
eg: We find the front cover of a book and open it the right way.
We follow the teacher's finger as it moves from left to right across a page.
We practise return sweep — going back to the start of the next line when we reach the end.
We point to the spaces between words and explain what they are for.
We hold a book that has been handed to us upside down and turn it the right way.
We track words from left to right while someone reads a big book to us.
